source_note: Keeper Rulebook Chapter 7 chase procedure. Movement actions from adjusted MOV relative to the slowest participant. Hazards, barriers, and conflicts resolve inside chase action economy; pushed rolls are not used in an active chase (p.134).

speed_roll.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p.132: each participant makes a CON roll (on foot) or Drive Auto roll (vehicle) to adjust MOV for the whole chase.

escape_criterion.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p.132: the chase is not played out if the fleeing adjusted MOV exceeds the pursuer's adjusted MOV.

starting_range.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p.133: default starting gap is 2 locations; 1 for a tenser chase; not advised beyond 2.

hazard.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p.134-135: cautious approach spends movement actions for bonus dice (1 action = 1 bonus die, max 2). Failed hazard check inflicts damage and 1D3 movement-action debt, but the character still advances to the next location.

barrier.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p.136-137: barriers block until negotiated or broken. Vehicles inflict Build×1D10 with no attack roll. Vehicle that fails to destroy the barrier is wrecked; destroyed barrier debris may become a hazard. Vehicle that destroys a barrier takes damage equal to half the barrier's HP prior to impact.

pedal_to_the_metal.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p.139-140: one movement action may move 2-5 locations. 2-3 locations → 1 penalty die on hazards; 4-5 locations → 2 penalty dice. Commitment cannot be retracted; stop on failed hazard.

sudden_hazards.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p.139: alternating Luck calls between players and Keeper. Success → caller places hazard; failure → other side places. Sudden hazards are Regular difficulty by default.

vehicles.source_note: Keeper Rulebook Table V (p.145): Drive Auto vehicles. MOV is chase MOV. Build is structural. Armor protects occupants vs external attacks. Passengers include driver capacity notes from the chart.

vehicular_collisions.source_note: Keeper Rulebook Table VI (p.147): collision severity tiers. Defaults: Regular hazard → minor, Hard → moderate, Extreme → severe (p.144). Each full 10 HP of damage decreases vehicle Build by 1 (p.145).

minor.label: Minor (fender-bender, low-speed bump)
minor.description: Low-speed bump; vehicle may be drivable. Passengers are jolted.

moderate.label: Moderate (side-swipe, clipped obstacle)
moderate.description: Significant impact; vehicle impaired but possibly moving. Passengers thrown about.

severe.label: Severe (head-on at speed, roll-over)
severe.description: Major structural damage; vehicle likely disabled. Passengers at risk of major wounds.

mayhem.label: Mayhem (high-speed crash, multi-vehicle)
mayhem.description: Catastrophic wreck; vehicle destroyed. Survival unlikely without restraint/luck.

roadkill.label: Roadkill (pedestrian / cyclist struck at speed)
roadkill.description: Vehicle strikes an unprotected person; the struck party suffers the bulk of the damage.
